title: podman search: display only name and description by default
body:
Change the default format of `podman search` to only display the name
and the description of each image.  The index is redundant to the name
and consumes a lot of space, and other descriptors (i.e., stars,
official, automated) are specific to Docker Hub and also consume a lot
space.  Users can still use `--format` for displaying the descriptors
they want to.

Also update the man page to account for the behavior and get some fresh
data in the examples.

Motivated by a recent conversation in libimage:
https://github.com/containers/common/pull/802#issuecomment-937108734

Signed-off-by: Valentin Rothberg <rothberg@redhat.com>

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *vrothberg* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [vrothberg] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
@mheon something worth highlighting in the 4.0 changelog.

LGTM

Why is the description truncated?
Can we get the full description and only truncate at the size of the terminal?  The descriptions seems to be truncted in the json file as well.
Ok, the --no-trunc field, seems a little bit less needed.
Seems like --no-trunc should be handled in the client, not in the library?

Playing around with your patch. I think having podman search work with a pager when it has a TTY would be cool, default to --no-trunc and page.

Basically the equivalent of ./bin/podman search ubi8 --no-trunc |less

> Playing around with your patch. I think having podman search work with a pager when it has a TTY would be cool, default to --no-trunc and page.
> 
> Basically the equivalent of ./bin/podman search ubi8 --no-trunc |less

I like the idea.  Could we break that into a separate issue?

Can we expand the descriptions out a bit, maybe to ~double what they're truncated to now? It seems like we're wasting a lot of space, given that we removed columns but kept the remaining ones the same size.
And can I request a `--legacy` or similar flag that displays the old output?

> And can I request a `--legacy` or similar flag that displays the old output?

`--legacy` or `--compatible` and keep the last three columns
The changes look OK to me, I'm just concerned about breaking Docker compatibility and or expected behavior.  Definitely, a change not to land before 4.0 in my book.

> The changes look OK to me, I'm just concerned about breaking Docker compatibility and or expected behavior. Definitely, a change not to land before 4.0 in my book.

Agreed on 4.0. Regarding compat: We never really were Docker compatible as Docker doesn't display the `INDEX` column.
Updated.  I let @jwhonce take care of truncation in #11924.
LGTM, I think some tests are flaking.
